## Overview
Complete all pending blog post drafts for the Cryptography course. These posts document key cryptographic concepts, security principles, and implementation details covered throughout the semester.

## Course Information
- **Course**: Cryptography
- **Semester**: Spring 2026
- **Status**: Multiple draft posts pending completion

   - Priority: High (foundational)

2. **Symmetric Encryption Basics** (`2026-01-19-cryptography-symmetric-encryption-basics.md`)

5. **Attack Models and Security Goals** (`2026-01-26-cryptography-attack-models-and-security-goals.md`)
   - Status: Draft

6. **Public Key Cryptography Overview** (`2026-01-28-cryptography-public-key-cryptography-overview.md`)
   - Status: Draft

7. **TBD Topic from Canvas Modules** (`2026-01-30-cryptography-tbd-topic-from-canvas-modules.md`)
   - Status: Draft
   - Topics: To be determined from course materials

## Requirements

### Content Standards
- [ ] Each post follows Jekyll frontmatter format
- [ ] Reading time approximately 15 minutes (±2 min)
- [ ] Word count: 2000-2500 words
- [ ] All mathematical notation properly formatted (KaTeX)
- [ ] Code examples syntax-highlighted (if applicable)
- [ ] Diagrams created/described for complex concepts
- [ ] External resources included (3-5 per post)

### Quality Checklist
- [ ] All topics from lecture/source materials covered
- [ ] Concepts explained clearly with examples
- [ ] Step-by-step explanations for algorithms
- [ ] Real-world applications and use cases
- [ ] Security considerations and best practices
- [ ] References to course textbook and external resources
- [ ] Cross-references to related blog posts

### Technical Requirements
- [ ] Frontmatter includes correct course tag: `course: "Cryptography"`
- [ ] Proper categories and tags assigned
- [ ] Excerpt written (50-100 words)
- [ ] Date matches lecture/class date
- [ ] Draft flag removed before publishing

## Resources

### Course Materials
- Lecture PDFs and slides
- Course textbook: *Introduction to Modern Cryptography* (Katz & Lindell)
- Canvas modules and assignments
- Additional reading materials

### Reference Files
- `BLOG_POST_GENERATION_INSTRUCTIONS.md` - Blog post format guidelines
- `_data/courses.yaml` - Course metadata
- `math-rules.md` - Mathematical notation standards
- `_posts/` - Existing published posts for style reference

### External Resources
- Cryptography textbooks (listed in draft files)
- Academic papers and research articles
- Industry standards (NIST, RFC documents)

## Deliverables

1. **Completed Blog Posts**: All 7 draft files converted to publication-ready posts
2. **Moved to `_posts/`**: Draft files moved from `_drafts/` to `_posts/` directory
3. **Quality Review**: All posts reviewed against quality checklist
4. **Testing**: Posts tested locally with Jekyll build
5. **Documentation**: Update any course index or navigation if needed

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All 7 blog posts completed and publication-ready
- [ ] All posts pass quality checklist
- [ ] All posts build successfully with Jekyll
- [ ] Mathematical notation renders correctly
- [ ] Code examples (if any) are properly formatted
- [ ] External links are valid and accessible
- [ ] Posts are moved from `_drafts/` to `_posts/`
- [ ] No draft flags remaining in frontmatter

## Notes

- Some draft files contain stubs that need full content development
- Coordinate with course schedule to ensure topics are covered in order
- Consider creating a course index page if multiple posts exist
- Ensure consistency in terminology and notation across all posts
